perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party hereby agrees that the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall (a) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, (b) hold the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in trust and keep it confidential, (c) use the same means they use to protect their own confidential information, but in any event not less than reasonable means, to prevent the disclosure by them and any of th eir Representatives of the Confidential Information communicated to them by the Disclosing Party and its Representatives, and (d) not copy or reproduce, or permit to be copied or reproduced, in any way, any part of the Confidential Information of the Discl osing Party except in accordance with and for the purposes set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party hereby agrees that the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall (a) use the Confidential Information primarily for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, (b) endeavor to hold the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in confidence, (c) use what they consider appropriate means to prevent the disclosure by them and any of their Representatives of the Confidential Information communicated to them by the Disclosing Party and its Representatives, and (d) may copy or reproduce parts of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party with reasonable justification.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'solely' to 'primarily,' 'hold in trust and keep it confidential' to 'endeavor to hold in confidence,' 'not less than reasonable means' to 'what they consider appropriate means,' and adding 'may' to the copying clause with 'reasonable justification', the obligations become significantly weaker and more subjective. This introduces ambiguity and makes enforcement more difficult.
perturbation.law_explanation: PUTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets. The modified text replaces strict obligations with weaker, subjective standards ('primarily,' 'endeavor,' 'what they consider appropriate,' 'reasonable justification'), potentially failing to meet the 'reasonable efforts' standard required for trade secret protection under Pennsylvania law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 12 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes § 5304 (2022) - Damages :: 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Penn. Cons. Stat. 2023 Penn. Cons. Stat. 2022 Penn. Cons. Stat. (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated & Unconsolidated Statutes Title 12 - COMMERCE AND TRADE Chapter 53 - Trade Secrets Section 5304 - Damages Universal Citation: 12 PA Cons Stat § 5304 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next § 5304. Damages. (a) Monetary damages. --Except to the extent that a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation renders a monetary recovery inequitable, a complainant is entitled to recover damages for misappropriation. Damages can include both the actual loss caused by misappropriation and the unjust enrichment caused by misappropriation that is not taken into account in computing actual loss. In lieu of damages measured by any other methods, the damages caused by misappropriation may be measured by imposition of liability for a reasonable royalty for a misappropriator's unauthorized disclosure or use of a trade secret. (b) Exemplary damages. --If willful and malicious misappropriation exists, the court may award exemplary damages in an amount not exceeding twice any award made under subsection (a). Previous Next Disclaimer: These codes may not be the most recent version. Pennsylvania may have more current or accurate information. perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligations related to maintaining the confidentiality of trade secrets. The original text used terms like 'solely,' 'hold in trust and keep it confidential,' and 'not less than reasonable means,' indicating a strong commitment to confidentiality. The changed text uses weaker, more subjective terms like 'primarily,' 'endeavor to hold in confidence,' and 'what they consider appropriate means.' This shift from strict obligations to subjective standards could fail to meet the 'reasonable efforts' standard required for trade secret protection, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 2, 12 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes § 5304 (2022), discusses damages for misappropriation, implying a legal framework for protecting trade secrets. The modifications introduce ambiguity, making it difficult to enforce confidentiality and potentially undermining trade secret protection, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In any event, the Receiving Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives , and each Party agrees, at its sole expense, to take commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Repr esentatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.changed_text: In any event, the Receiving Party might be responsible for a breach of this Agreement by its Representatives based on the circumstances, and each Party will consider measures to encourage its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be responsible' to 'might be responsible' and 'take commercially reasonable measures' to 'will consider measures to encourage' significantly weakens the legal obligation. The change makes responsibility and preventative measures uncertain and discretionary, creating ambiguity and reducing the enforceability of the clause.
perturbation.law_explanation: PUTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets, including taking steps to prevent unauthorized disclosure by employees and agents. The modified text, by making responsibility and preventative measures discretionary ('might be responsible', 'will consider measures to encourage'), potentially fails to meet the 'reasonable efforts' standard required for trade secret protection under Pennsylvania law, as it weakens the obligation to actively prevent disclosure.

perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the Receiving Party's obligation to protect confidential information. The original text states the Receiving Party 'shall be responsible' and 'take commercially reasonable measures.' The changed text softens this to 'might be responsible' and 'will consider measures to encourage,' making the protection of confidential information discretionary. The Law Explanation states that PUTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets. 12 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes § 5304 discusses damages for misappropriation, implying a legal expectation to prevent such misappropriation. The modified text's discretionary language potentially fails to meet the 'reasonable efforts' standard, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees to give the other Party prompt notice of any violation of this A greement, including, without limitation, any unauthorized disclosure or use of the other Party’s Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees to consider giving the other Party notice of a potential violation of this Agreement, including any possible unauthorized disclosure or use of the other Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'consider giving' and 'possible unauthorized disclosure' make the action of notification discretionary and weakens the certainty of reporting violations. This introduces ambiguity, potentially undermining the enforcement of confidentiality.
perturbation.law_explanation: PUTSA implicitly requires a proactive approach to protecting trade secrets. Failure to promptly report known or suspected breaches can be construed as a failure to take reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of the information. The modified text, which makes reporting discretionary ('consider giving') and reduces the certainty of the disclosure ('possible unauthorized disclosure'), could be seen as a failure to fulfill the reasonable efforts required by PUTSA, jeopardizing the trade secret status of the information.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces 'agrees to give the other Party prompt notice of any violation' with 'agrees to consider giving the other Party notice of a potential violation'. This change introduces ambiguity and makes the notification discretionary. The Law Explanation suggests that PUTSA requires a proactive approach to protecting trade secrets and failure to promptly report suspected breaches can be construed as a failure to take reasonable steps. The original text mandated prompt notification, aligning with a proactive approach. The modified text weakens this obligation, making it discretionary and impacting the enforceability of protecting confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by a Party or any of its Representatives and that the other Party shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach (without the need to post any bond in connection therewith) . Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by a Party of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all oth er remedies available at law or equity to such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood that money damages may not always be a sufficient remedy for any alleged breach of this Agreement by a Party or its Representatives, and that the other Party may consider seeking equitable relief, potentially including injunction and specific performance, as one possible remedy for any such alleged breach. Such remedies may or may not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'would not be a sufficient remedy' with 'may not always be a sufficient remedy', 'shall be entitled to seek' with 'may consider seeking', 'including injunction and specific performance' with 'potentially including injunction and specific performance', and 'shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies' with 'may or may not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies', the certainty of equitable relief is undermined. This introduces ambiguity regarding the availability and scope of remedies.
perturbation.law_explanation: PUTSA explicitly allows for injunctive relief to prevent misappropriation of trade secrets. By making injunctive relief discretionary ('may consider seeking', 'potentially including'), the modified text contradicts the intent of PUTSA, which seeks to provide strong protection for trade secrets through equitable remedies, as it casts doubt on the availability of this critical remedy.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 12 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes § 5303 (2022) - Injunctive relief :: 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Penn. Cons. Stat. 2023 Penn. Cons. Stat. 2022 Penn. Cons. Stat. (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated & Unconsolidated Statutes Title 12 - COMMERCE AND TRADE Chapter 53 - Trade Secrets Section 5303 - Injunctive relief Universal Citation: 12 PA Cons Stat § 5303 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next § 5303. Injunctive relief. (a) Injunctions. --Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined. Upon application to the court, an injunction shall be terminated when the trade secret has ceased to exist, but the injunction may be continued for an additional reasonable period of time in order to eliminate commercial advantage that otherwise would be derived from the misappropriation. (b) Exceptional circumstances. --In exceptional circumstances, an injunction may condition future use upon payment of a reasonable royalty for no longer than the period of time for which use could have been prohibited. Exceptional circumstances include, but are not limited to, a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation that renders a prohibitive injunction inequitable. (c) Affirmative acts compelled by court order. --In appropriate circumstances, affirmative acts to protect a trade secret may be compelled by court order. Please check official sources. This site is pro
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity regarding injunctive relief, stating the other party 'may consider seeking equitable relief, potentially including injunction and specific performance, as one possible remedy,' and that such remedies 'may or may not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies.' This contradicts the intent of Pennsylvania law, specifically 12 PA Cons Stat § 5303 (2022), which states that 'Actual or threatened misappropriation may be enjoined.' The original text mandated equitable relief, while the changed text makes it discretionary. The law snippet provides a direct mandate for injunctive relief, which the changed text undermines by making it optional.
